intro:
There is no information available about the plant Oxydectes metallica. However, based on the name, it is possible that it is a made-up or fictional plant name.

But, if we assume that this plant is related to Oxydendrum arboreum (Sweetgum), I can provide information about it.

Oxydendrum arboreum is a plant species also known as the Sweetgum tree. It is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark and leaves of the Sweetgum tree have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress.

The bioactive constituents of the Sweetgum tree include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from the Sweetgum tree include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
